facts.text: The first organized cheerleading took place at the University of Minnesota in 1898 when student Johnny Campbell stood before the crowd and directed a unified chant.
The Dallas Cowboys Cheerleaders (DCC) were founded in 1972 and quickly became the most iconic cheerleading squad in professional sports.
A basket toss is a stunt where flyers are launched into the air by bases and can reach heights of over 20 feet.
The Netflix documentary series Cheer, which premiered in January 2020, followed Navarro College's competitive cheer team and became a massive cultural phenomenon.
Cheerleading has one of the highest catastrophic injury rates among female sports, with most serious injuries resulting from stunts and tumbling.
The movie Bring It On, released in 2000, grossed over $90 million worldwide and spawned five sequels.
Former U.S. Presidents George W. Bush, Dwight D. Eisenhower, and Franklin D. Roosevelt were all cheerleaders in their school years.
The UCA (Universal Cheerleaders Association) was founded by Jeff Webb in 1974 to provide training camps and competitions for cheerleaders.
Top Gun Large Coed, based in Miami, Florida, is one of the most decorated all-star cheerleading teams in the world.
A liberty is a single-leg stunt where the flyer stands on one foot in the base's hands with the other leg bent at the knee.
The Cheerleading Worlds, held annually in Orlando, Florida, is considered the most prestigious competition in all-star cheerleading.
The ICU (International Cheer Union) was founded in 2004 and now has member federations in over 110 countries.
Super Bowl I on January 15, 1967, featured cheerleaders from both the Green Bay Packers and Kansas City Chiefs on the sidelines.
A standing tuck is a backflip performed from a standing position without a running start, and it is a fundamental tumbling skill in competitive cheerleading.
The SEC (Southeastern Conference) is known for having some of the most spirited and competitive college cheerleading programs in the country.
Japan has one of the largest cheerleading communities outside the United States, with over 2 million participants.
Cheer Athletics, based in Plano, Texas, is one of the largest and most successful all-star cheerleading gyms in the world.
The scorpion is an advanced flexibility stunt where the flyer grabs her foot behind her head, creating a shape resembling a scorpion's tail.
Pom routines are a style of dance team performance that incorporates pom-poms and synchronize sharp, precise movements to music.
The NCA (National Cheerleaders Association) was founded in 1948 by Lawrence Herkimer, who also invented the spirit stick and the herkie jump.
Dallas Cowboys Cheerleaders: Making the Team is a reality TV show that has aired on CMT since 2006, following the intense DCC audition process.
A full-up is a stunt where the flyer is tossed into the air by the bases and completes a full 360-degree rotation before being caught in a liberty or other position.
Cheerleading was originally an all-male activity, and women didn't begin participating until the 1920s.
The Laker Girls, the dance team of the Los Angeles Lakers, were founded in 1979 and are one of the most recognized NBA dance teams.
Varsity Spirit, founded by Jeff Webb in 1974, is the largest cheerleading company in the world, running competitions, camps, and producing uniforms.
The first cheerleading competition was held in 1978 and was broadcast on CBS, helping legitimize cheerleading as a competitive sport.
Stingray Allstars, based in Marietta, Georgia, has produced multiple Cheerleading Worlds champions across various divisions.
Super Bowl III on January 12, 1969, is famous for Joe Namath's guarantee, and the Baltimore Colts cheerleaders performed at the Orange Bowl.
A layout is a tumbling skill where the gymnast performs a backflip with a fully extended, straight body position.
The University of Kentucky has won more UCA College Cheerleading National Championships than any other school, with over 20 titles.
In all-star cheerleading, teams are divided into levels 1 through 6, with Level 6 allowing the most advanced stunts, tumbling, and tosses.
Cheerleading squads at NFL games typically perform choreographed dance routines, sideline cheers, and community outreach.
The first known use of pom-poms in cheerleading was in the 1930s, and they were originally made of paper.
The ESPN College Cheerleading Championship has been a nationally televised event since the 1980s, showcasing the top college squads.
Spotters are required during all stunts in competitive cheerleading to help prevent injuries if a flyer falls.
The ICU World Cheerleading Championships have been held annually since 2004, with the United States dominating the medal count.
A pike jump is performed by lifting both legs straight out in front of the body while reaching for the toes at the peak of the jump.
Navarro College in Corsicana, Texas, has won 14 NCA Junior College National Championships, making them a dynasty in two-year college cheer.
The Dallas Cowboys Cheerleaders iconic white shorts and blue top uniform was designed by Paula Van Wagoner in 1972.
Cheerleading-related injuries account for roughly 66% of all catastrophic injuries in female high school and college athletics.
Super Bowl XX on January 26, 1986, saw the Chicago Bears dominate the New England Patriots 46-10, with the Super Bowl Shuffle becoming a cultural phenomenon.
A toe touch is one of the most recognizable cheerleading jumps, where the legs extend out to the sides while the arms form a T shape.
The Philadelphia Eagles were the last NFL team to add an official cheerleading squad, debuting them in 1984.
All-star cheerleading gyms typically train athletes year-round, with competition season running from December through May.
The herkie jump, named after Lawrence Herkimer, involves one leg extended straight and the other bent beneath the body.
In 2016, the International Olympic Committee granted the ICU provisional recognition, putting cheerleading on a path toward potential Olympic inclusion.
The Baltimore Ravens cheerleaders, known as the Ravens Cheerleaders, have performed at two Super Bowl victories.
A pyramid in cheerleading involves multiple stunt groups building a connected formation, often reaching two and a half body lengths high.
The University of Alabama's cheerleading squad is one of the most visible in college football, performing at some of the most-watched games each season.
Johnny Campbell is recognized as the first cheerleader, leading the chant 'Rah, Rah, Rah! Ski-U-Mah!' at a Minnesota football game in 1898.
Spirit Innovations, founded in 2000, is one of many event producers that run regional and national cheerleading competitions across the country.
A back handspring is a fundamental tumbling skill where the cheerleader jumps backward, lands on their hands, and flips over onto their feet.
Super Bowl XLII on February 3, 2008, featured the New York Giants' stunning upset of the undefeated New England Patriots.
The first Bring It On movie was inspired by real competitive cheerleading rivalries and was praised for bringing attention to the sport.
Male cheerleaders at the college level serve primarily as bases and are essential for executing advanced partner stunts and pyramids.
The AACCA (American Association of Cheerleading Coaches and Administrators) establishes safety rules and guidelines for school-based cheerleading.
Cheer Athletics Wildcats is one of the most dominant Senior Large All Girl teams in the history of the Cheerleading Worlds.
The Indianapolis Colts Cheerleaders have been performing since 1984 and are one of the most active squads in community engagement.
Tumbling passes in competitive cheerleading typically include a combination of back handsprings, tucks, layouts, and fulls.
The University of Louisville's coed cheerleading team has won multiple UCA National Championships and is considered a powerhouse program.
Cheerleading became increasingly female-dominated during World War II when men left for military service and women took over the squads.
The term 'flyer' refers to the person at the top of a stunt who is lifted and supported by the bases and back spot.
Super Bowl LI on February 5, 2017, included the greatest comeback in Super Bowl history when the New England Patriots overcame a 28-3 deficit against the Atlanta Falcons.
The USASF (U.S. All Star Federation) governs all-star cheerleading in the United States and oversees the rules for the Cheerleading Worlds.
A cradle is a dismount where the flyer is caught by the bases in a cradling position after performing a skill in the air.
The New England Patriots Cheerleaders were officially organized in 1977 and have performed at six Super Bowl victories.
The first professional cheerleading squad was the Baltimore Colts cheerleaders in the 1950s, though they were a much more traditional squad than modern NFL teams.
Competitive cheerleading routines are typically two minutes and 30 seconds long and include stunts, tumbling, jumps, and a dance section.
The University of Central Florida's cheerleading program has won multiple national championships across various divisions.
Coach Monica Aldama of Navarro College became a household name after the Netflix series Cheer showcased her coaching style and the team's quest for a national title.
A heel stretch is a stunt where the flyer extends one leg straight up while holding her foot near her head, demonstrating extreme flexibility.
The Miami Dolphins Cheerleaders were one of the first NFL squads to adopt a more dance-oriented style in the 1970s.
The Cheerleading Worlds are produced by Varsity Spirit and take place at the ESPN Wide World of Sports Complex at Walt Disney World in Orlando.
Super Bowl XLIX on February 1, 2015, ended with Malcolm Butler's goal-line interception sealing a Patriots victory over the Seattle Seahawks.
A round-off is a cartwheel-like tumbling skill that finishes with both feet landing together, and it is used to initiate tumbling passes.
The Green Bay Packers did not have an official cheerleading squad for most of their history and are one of the few NFL teams without one.
The World Cheerleading Championships organized by the ICU feature national teams from dozens of countries competing in various divisions.
Bring It On: All or Nothing, the third film in the franchise, starred Hayden Panettiere and Solange Knowles.
All-star cheerleading Level 1 is designed for beginners and restricts stunts to basic level, prep-level heights.
The San Francisco 49ers Gold Rush is one of the most recognizable NFL dance/cheerleading squads, known for their halftime performances.
A double full is an advanced tumbling skill involving two complete 360-degree twists while performing a backflip.
The NCAA does not officially recognize cheerleading as a varsity sport, which affects scholarship allocation and Title IX compliance.
Super Bowl XXXVI on February 3, 2002, launched the New England Patriots dynasty when Tom Brady led a game-winning drive against the St. Louis Rams.
Spirit fingers — rapidly wiggling fingers with outstretched hands — were popularized and parodied in the movie Bring It On.
The University of Michigan cheerleading squad performs at one of the largest college football stadiums in the country, seating over 107,000 fans.
A back spot is the person who stands behind a stunt group and is responsible for supporting the flyer's head and shoulders.
The Denver Broncos Cheerleaders have performed at three Super Bowl victories, showcasing their routines on football's biggest stage.
Cheer on Netflix received six Emmy nominations in 2020, bringing unprecedented mainstream attention to competitive cheerleading.
The concept of organized chanting at sports events dates back to the 1860s at Princeton University, making it a precursor to modern cheerleading.
A cupie (or awesome) is a stunt where the flyer stands on one foot in a single base's hand with arms raised overhead.
The Pittsburgh Steelers did not have official cheerleaders from 1969 to 2020, famously being one of the last NFL teams to go without.
All-star gyms like World Cup Allstars in New Jersey have produced multiple Worlds champions and are considered elite training facilities.
The rewind is an advanced technique where the flyer does a back tuck into a stunt without being tossed by the bases.
Super Bowl XLIV on February 7, 2010, was the New Orleans Saints' first championship, a victory that became symbolic of the city's recovery from Hurricane Katrina.
Lawrence 'Herkie' Herkimer founded the first cheerleading camp in 1948, and his innovations shaped the modern cheerleading industry.
A tick-tock is a stunt transition where the flyer switches from standing on one foot to the other while remaining elevated.
The Washington Commanders (formerly Redskins) cheerleaders, known as the First Ladies of Football, were established in 1962.
Competitive cheerleading scoring is based on difficulty, execution, and overall performance, with deductions for falls, bobbles, and safety violations.
The University of Georgia cheerleading squad features Uga, the live bulldog mascot, and is a fixture of SEC football traditions.
Brazil has one of the fastest-growing cheerleading communities in South America, with numerous all-star teams competing internationally.
A whip is a tumbling skill similar to a back handspring but performed without the hands touching the ground.
The Dallas Cowboys Cheerleaders receive thousands of applications each year, but only about 36 women make the final squad.
Super Bowl XLVI on February 5, 2012, saw the New York Giants defeat the New England Patriots for the second time in five years.
Cheerleading at the high school level includes approximately 3.5 million participants in the United States.
The Kansas City Chiefs Cheerleaders are one of the longest-running NFL squads, having been founded in 1963.
A front walkover is a flexibility-based tumbling skill where the athlete moves from standing into a bridge and through to standing while going forward.
The STUNT format, developed by USA Cheer, is designed to gain NCAA recognition by emphasizing the athletic and competitive elements of cheerleading.
The Cincinnati Bengals Ben-Gals have won multiple Pro Bowl cheerleading selections and are known for their high-energy performances.
An arabesque in cheerleading is a pose where the flyer extends one leg straight behind her while balancing on the other.
Finland has a strong competitive cheerleading scene and has produced top-ranking teams at the ICU World Championships.
The Tampa Bay Buccaneers Cheerleaders have performed at two Super Bowl home games, a rare occurrence in NFL history.
A tuck jump involves pulling the knees up to the chest while in the air, and it is both a jump and a tumbling fundamental.
Super Bowl LVII on February 12, 2023, featured the Kansas City Chiefs defeating the Philadelphia Eagles in a closely contested game.
Cheerleading uniforms have evolved from long skirts and sweaters in the early 1900s to modern athletic wear designed for performance and flexibility.
The Carolina Panthers TopCats are known for their community service programs and their performances at Bank of America Stadium.
USA Cheer was founded in 2007 as the national governing body for cheerleading in the United States.
The double-down is a dismount where the flyer does two twists while coming down from a stunt, caught by the bases in a cradle.
Morehead State University's coed cheerleading team has won multiple national titles and is a dominant force in college cheerleading.
The Houston Texans Cheerleaders were founded in 2002 when the franchise debuted and have grown into one of the most popular squads in the NFL.
In all-star cheerleading, the Large Senior Coed division is often considered the most competitive and entertaining division at the Worlds.
A kip-up (or kick-up) is a move where the cheerleader goes from a supine position on the ground to standing in one explosive motion.
The Cleveland Browns had cheerleaders called the Brownettes until 1969, went without them for decades, and have not reinstated them.
Cheer camps run by UCA and NCA each summer train tens of thousands of high school and college cheerleaders across the country.
Super Bowl IV on January 11, 1970, was the last Super Bowl before the AFL-NFL merger, with the Kansas City Chiefs defeating the Minnesota Vikings.
The bow and arrow is a stunt where the flyer pulls one leg up beside her head while the other leg remains on the base's hands.
The Atlanta Falcons Cheerleaders have been performing since 1966 and are one of the original NFL cheerleading squads.
Competitive cheerleading requires athletes to master multiple disciplines, including gymnastics, dance, stunting, and crowd engagement.
The University of Oklahoma's cheerleading squad is famous for riding the Sooner Schooner onto the field before games.
A standing back handspring is performed from a stationary position and is a milestone skill for cheerleaders at every level.
The Minnesota Vikings Cheerleaders, known as the MVC, were established in 1984 and perform at U.S. Bank Stadium.
All-star cheerleading teams compete in divisions based on age, size, and gender composition, including Tiny, Mini, Youth, Junior, and Senior levels.
The switch leap combines a jump with a split-leg transition, showing flexibility and control in midair.
Spirit Rally Tough (SRT) mats and spring floors are now required at most competition venues to reduce impact injuries.
Super Bowl XXIII on January 22, 1989, ended with Joe Montana throwing a game-winning touchdown to John Taylor with 34 seconds left.
George W. Bush was a cheerleader at Phillips Academy Andover and later at Yale University.
The Tennessee Titans Cheerleaders perform at Nissan Stadium and are known for their dance-heavy performances.
A sponge toss is an entry-level toss where the bases start with their hands beneath the flyer's feet to launch her upward.
The University of Texas cheerleading squad has a tradition of performing 'Hook 'Em Horns' gestures during every game.
Germany has a growing competitive cheerleading community, with multiple teams competing at the European Cheerleading Championships.
A hitch kick involves a series of alternating knee lifts performed in the air, showcasing control and timing.
The New Orleans Saints Saintsations were renamed the Krewe in 2022, reflecting modern branding while maintaining their legacy.
In competitive cheerleading, a zero deduction routine — sometimes called a 'hit' routine — means no errors were committed during the performance.
A pike basket toss involves the flyer performing a pike position at the peak of a basket toss before being caught.
The Jacksonville Jaguars ROAR is the team's cheerleading squad, known for their community outreach and dance performances.
Super Bowl XXV on January 27, 1991, was decided by Scott Norwood's missed field goal, giving the New York Giants a 20-19 victory over the Buffalo Bills.
Cheerleading glitter bows became a signature look in competitive cheer, and many teams customize their bows to match uniforms.
The Los Angeles Rams Cheerleaders were reestablished when the team returned to Los Angeles in 2016 after two decades in St. Louis.
The University of Florida's cheerleading squad regularly competes for national titles and is a top SEC program.
A full twist layout is a tumbling skill combining a fully extended backflip with a 360-degree twist.
The International Cheer Union held its first official World Championship in 2004 in Miyazaki, Japan.
Cheer Athletics Cheetahs, a Senior Small All Girl team from Texas, has been one of the most dominant teams in its division.
The Arizona Cardinals Cheerleaders are one of the squads that audition hundreds of candidates each spring for about 30 roster spots.
A prep is a two-legged stunt where the flyer stands at chest height on the bases' hands, one of the most basic stunt positions.
The University of Alabama Birmingham (UAB) has a competitive cheerleading program that competes at the college national championship level.
Male cheerleaders in all-star coed teams perform many of the same tumbling skills as female athletes, including standing fulls and double fulls.
Cheerleading has grown steadily in the United Kingdom, with the UK Cheerleading Association hosting annual national championships.
A front tuck is a forward-rotating flip with the knees pulled to the chest, requiring significant power off the takeoff.
Super Bowl XXXII on January 25, 1998, saw the Denver Broncos end the NFC's 13-game winning streak with a victory over the Green Bay Packers.
The Detroit Lions Cheerleaders were disbanded in 2007 due to budget cuts and have not been reinstated.
Dwight D. Eisenhower was a cheerleader at Abilene High School in Kansas before attending West Point.
A ground-up liberty is a stunt that starts from the ground and goes directly into a single-leg liberty position at extension.
The Penn State cheerleading squad performs for crowds of over 106,000 at Beaver Stadium, one of the largest venues in college football.
The IASF (International All Star Federation) works alongside the USASF to establish and maintain rules for all-star cheerleading worldwide.
Top Gun Allstars TGLC (Top Gun Large Coed) is one of the most followed and successful teams on the Cheerleading Worlds stage.
The Seattle Seahawks Sea Gals were rebranded as the Seattle Seahawks Dancers in 2021 to better reflect their dance-focused performance style.
A whip back is a no-hands tumbling skill that generates momentum for subsequent skills in a tumbling pass.
Cheerleading competitions often use a panel scoring system where judges evaluate stunts, tumbling, jumps, dance, and overall impression independently.
The Buffalo Bills Jills were disbanded in 2014 after a lawsuit regarding pay and working conditions, highlighting ongoing labor issues in NFL cheerleading.
Super Bowl XXXIV on January 30, 2000, ended with Kevin Dyson being tackled one yard short of the end zone, sealing a St. Louis Rams victory.
A scale is a flexibility position where the cheerleader holds one leg extended high while standing on the other, creating a vertical line.
The Ohio State University cheerleading squad performs traditional cheers and stunts for crowds of over 100,000 at Ohio Stadium.
The concept of competitive cheerleading scoring was standardized in the 1980s, helping transform cheer from sideline activity to competitive sport.
Thailand has a strong presence in international cheerleading competitions, with several teams placing in the top ranks at the ICU Worlds.
A front handspring is a tumbling skill where the cheerleader springs forward, places their hands on the ground, and rotates over to their feet.
The San Diego (now LA) Charger Girls were established in 1961 and are one of the oldest continuously running cheerleading squads in the NFL.
Competitive cheerleading mats are required to be at least 42 feet by 54 feet for standard competition routines.
The Varsity All Star brand manages the end-of-season championships including NCA All-Star Nationals and the Cheerleading Worlds.
A heel stretch pull is a flexibility test often used in tryouts where the athlete pulls one leg up beside the ear while standing.
The New York Jets Flight Crew replaced the traditional Jets cheerleaders in 2007 with a more dance-focused squad.
Canada has a large and competitive cheerleading community, with the CCA (Cheer Canada Association) overseeing national teams.
Super Bowl XLVIII on February 2, 2014, was the first outdoor cold-weather Super Bowl, played at MetLife Stadium with the Seattle Seahawks dominating the Denver Broncos 43-8.
A show and go is a fake tumbling setup that transitions into a different skill or stunt, used to add creativity to routines.
The University of South Carolina cheerleading squad performs Sandstorm alongside 80,000 fans, creating one of the most electric atmospheres in college football.
Strength training is essential for cheerleading bases, who must be able to hold, toss, and catch flyers weighing up to 120 pounds or more.
The Baltimore Ravens Cheerleaders have performed at two Super Bowls, including the memorable Super Bowl XLVII power outage game.
The Cheerleading Worlds were first held in 2004 and have grown from a single division to dozens of divisions for teams of all ages and sizes.
A pencil drop is a dismount where the flyer falls straight down from a stunt position, keeping the body rigid while the bases catch.
Franklin D. Roosevelt was a cheerleader at Harvard University, where he cheered for the football team.
The Chicago Bears Honey Bears were disbanded in 1985, and the franchise has not had cheerleaders since, making them one of the few NFL teams without a squad.
All-star cheerleading Level 6 allows the most difficult skills, including double fulls in tumbling and rewinds to double-twisting dismounts.
The Liberty University cheerleading squad is known for performing at the largest college convocation in the country.
Australia has a rapidly growing cheerleading community, with the AASCF hosting annual national championships in multiple divisions.
A tic-toc switch-up combines a foot change with a position change while the flyer remains elevated at extension level.
Super Bowl LIV on February 2, 2020, saw the Kansas City Chiefs win their first Super Bowl in 50 years, defeating the San Francisco 49ers.
NCA All-Star Nationals, held annually in Dallas, Texas, is one of the largest all-star cheerleading competitions in the country.
The Miami Dolphins Cheerleaders routinely perform in some of the hottest conditions in the NFL, with game-day temperatures often exceeding 90 degrees.
A running tumbling pass typically starts with a round-off and progresses through back handsprings into a final skill like a full or double full.
The University of Mississippi (Ole Miss) cheerleading squad performs the 'Hotty Toddy' cheer, one of the most recognized college cheers in the South.
Cheer bows can be up to three inches wide and are considered an essential part of the competitive cheerleading uniform.
A ball-up is a stunt transition where the flyer is tossed from one stunt position and caught in another by the same or different bases.
The Las Vegas Raiders Raiderettes, founded in 1961, are one of the oldest professional cheerleading squads in the NFL.
In competitive cheerleading, a stunt sequence involves a connected series of stunts, transitions, and dismounts performed without pausing.
China has emerged as a competitor in international cheerleading, fielding strong national teams at ICU World Championships.
A C-jump is a jump where the body forms the shape of the letter C, with the back arched and arms reaching overhead.
Super Bowl LIII on February 3, 2019, was the lowest-scoring Super Bowl in history, with the New England Patriots defeating the Los Angeles Rams 13-3.
The Pop Warner organization includes cheerleading programs for youth athletes as young as five years old, introducing them to the sport early.
Many college cheerleading programs offer scholarships, though the exact amount and availability vary widely between schools and divisions.
A standing full is an advanced tumbling skill where the cheerleader performs a standing backflip with a 360-degree twist.
The Minnesota Vikings MVC (Minnesota Vikings Cheerleaders) have been a consistent presence at the team's home games since 1984.
The Spirit of Texas all-star gym, based in Bedford, Texas, has produced multiple World Championship teams across various divisions.
The concept of mascot cheerleading involves costumed characters performing crowd engagement and comedic routines alongside traditional cheerleaders.
A wolf wall is a stunt formation where multiple flyers stand on bases at different heights to create a layered visual effect.
The University of Oregon cheerleading squad performs alongside the Duck, one of the most famous college mascots in the country.
The first-ever cheerleading uniform consisted of a letter sweater and long pants or skirts, a far cry from modern athletic outfits.
A 2-2-1 pyramid is a common formation that has two stunt groups on the bottom, two at a middle level, and one flyer at the top.
Super Bowl X on January 18, 1976, was the first Super Bowl to be played at the Rose Bowl, with the Pittsburgh Steelers defeating the Dallas Cowboys.
The Prodigy all-star cheerleading gym, based in Florida, has earned multiple bids to the Cheerleading Worlds.
Competitive cheerleading practice schedules often involve 3-5 practices per week during competition season, each lasting 2-3 hours.
The JAMZ competition circuit is a popular competition producer that hosts events across the western United States.
A front aerial is an advanced tumbling skill — a front flip performed without the hands touching the ground.
The Pittsburgh Steelers introduced an official cheerleading squad in 2020 after over 50 years without one.
College cheerleading tryouts often test candidates on tumbling, jumps, stunting, crowd leading, and an interview component.
The Russian cheerleading federation has grown rapidly since the 2010s, with national teams competing at the ICU Worlds.
A twist cradle is a dismount from a stunt where the flyer performs one or more twists before landing in a cradle catch.
Super Bowl XIII on January 21, 1979, featured a rematch between the Dallas Cowboys and Pittsburgh Steelers, with the Steelers winning 35-31.
The American cheerleading market, including camps, competitions, apparel, and training, is estimated to be a multi-billion dollar industry.
The Arizona State University Sun Devil cheerleading squad performs in one of the largest college stadiums in the Pac-12.
A shoulder sit is a basic partner stunt where the flyer sits on the shoulders of a base, often used in youth and beginner cheerleading.
The USASF Cheerleading Worlds divide teams into divisions by age, team size, and gender (All Girl or Coed).
Competitive cheerleading seasons typically culminate in April and May with national and world championship events.
The Miami Heat dance team, founded in 1988, is one of the most prominent NBA entertainment squads outside of traditional cheerleading.
A flatback is a catching position used by bases where they extend their arms to create a flat surface for the flyer to land on.
The University of Tennessee's cheerleading squad leads 100,000 fans in the 'Rocky Top' cheer, creating one of the loudest atmospheres in college sports.
Mexico has fielded competitive cheerleading teams at the ICU World Championships, with growing participation across the country.
A combination tumbling pass includes both standing and running elements connected together in one continuous sequence.
Super Bowl LV on February 7, 2021, saw Tom Brady and the Tampa Bay Buccaneers defeat the Kansas City Chiefs at Raymond James Stadium.
The concept of 'mat talk' in competitive cheer refers to coaches and teammates yelling encouragement and cues from the edge of the performance floor.
A needle is an extreme flexibility stunt where the flyer pulls one leg straight up behind her body into a near-vertical split while standing on one foot.
The Dallas Cowboys Cheerleaders have traveled to perform for U.S. military troops overseas through USO tours since the 1970s.
The University of North Carolina cheerleading squad performs at the Dean Smith Center, one of the most historic basketball arenas in the country.
The World Cheerleading Association (WCA) was one of the early organizations to create competitive events for cheerleading teams.
A handstand is a foundational skill in cheerleading that develops the arm strength and body control needed for advanced tumbling.
The Los Angeles Charger Girls use a fast-paced, dance-heavy style typical of West Coast NFL cheerleading squads.
The longest-running college cheerleading rivalry is arguably between Ohio State and Michigan, where cheerleaders have faced off for over a century.
All-star cheerleading registration fees for a competition season can range from $2,000 to $5,000 per athlete, including competition entry fees and uniforms.
A braced flip is a pyramid-level skill where the flyer performs a flip while connected to other flyers or bases in a formation.
Super Bowl XXII on January 31, 1988, saw Doug Williams become the first Black quarterback to win a Super Bowl, leading the Washington Redskins to a 42-10 victory.
The concept of 'bid day' in all-star cheerleading refers to when teams earn their invitation to compete at the Cheerleading Worlds through qualifying events.
South Korea has a vibrant cheerleading culture, particularly at baseball games, where professional cheerleaders perform choreographed routines.
A thigh stand is a basic partner stunt where the flyer stands on the thighs of two bases who are in a lunge position.
The University of Memphis cheerleading squad has a rich tradition of energetic performances at FedExForum and Liberty Bowl Memorial Stadium.
The first DCC audition process was documented on the reality show Making the Team in 2006, and it has aired continuously since.

The St. Louis Rams cheerleaders transitioned to the Los Angeles Rams cheerleaders when the team relocated in 2016.
Professional cheerleading tryouts in the NFL typically span multiple rounds over several weeks, evaluating dance, fitness, interviews, and public speaking.
The University of Southern California (USC) Song Girls are technically a dance squad rather than cheerleaders, but they are among the most famous sideline performers in college sports.
A pop cradle is a quick dismount from a prep-level stunt where the flyer is popped up slightly before being caught in a cradle.
The Green Bay Packers' lack of a cheerleading squad is partly attributed to the community-owned nature of the franchise and a focus on tradition.
Competitive cheerleading teams known as 'open' teams allow athletes of any age above a minimum, adding flexibility to team composition.
Colombia has produced strong cheerleading teams that compete at the Pan American Cheerleading Championships and ICU Worlds.
A split leap is a jump where the cheerleader extends both legs into a full split position while airborne.
Super Bowl LVI on February 13, 2022, was played at SoFi Stadium in Los Angeles, with the Rams defeating the Cincinnati Bengals.
The concept of 'full outs' in practice refers to running an entire competition routine from start to finish without stopping.
A ball-up full is a stunt transition where the flyer is tossed, performs a full twist, and is caught in a new stunt position.
The Philadelphia Eagles Cheerleaders, established in 1984, perform complex dance routines and participate in extensive community programs.
The UCA College National Championship is held annually in January in Orlando, Florida, and is one of the most prestigious college cheer events.
Gabi Butler, who became famous through the Netflix series Cheer, has trained at multiple all-star gyms and has a massive social media following.
A chair sit is a basic partner stunt where the flyer sits in a seated position on the hands of a base.
The San Diego State Aztecs cheerleading program competes in the NCAA division and has earned national recognition.
All-star cheerleading music is custom mixed and typically costs $500 to $2,000 per routine, featuring licensed music and voiceovers.
A suicide (or dead man) is a crowd-style trick where a cheerleader falls face-first and is caught by teammates at the last moment.
The Dallas Cowboys Cheerleaders were the first NFL cheerleading squad to release a swimsuit calendar, which became an annual tradition.
The University of Iowa cheerleading squad leads the wave at Kinnick Stadium, a beloved tradition at Iowa Hawkeyes football games.
The Cheerleading Worlds 2019 was one of the most-watched events in all-star history, with live streaming reaching millions of viewers.
A reload is a stunt where the flyer comes down from extension to prep level before being pushed back up.
Super Bowl LVIII on February 11, 2024, saw the Kansas City Chiefs defeat the San Francisco 49ers in overtime in Las Vegas.
The National Federation of State High School Associations (NFHS) reports that cheerleading participation has remained steady at around 400,000 high school athletes annually.
The Tampa Bay Buccaneers Cheerleaders performed at Raymond James Stadium during the franchise's two Super Bowl victories.
A double-twist basket toss involves the flyer performing two complete rotational twists at the peak of a basket toss.
The University of Notre Dame cheerleading squad appears at one of the most storied programs in college football, with traditions dating back over a century.
Cheerleading shoes are specially designed with flat soles, arch support, and lightweight construction to optimize both tumbling and stunting performance.
A shoulder stand is a stunt where the flyer stands on the shoulders of a base, one of the first extension-level skills cheerleaders learn.

The NCA National Championship, held annually in Dallas, is one of the longest-running cheerleading competitions in the United States.
A cartwheel is the first tumbling skill most cheerleaders learn and is the building block for round-offs and aerials.
The concept of 'hitting zero' means a team performed its routine with no deductions — the ultimate goal of every competition performance.
New Zealand has seen rapid growth in cheerleading, with the NZCA organizing national championships and sending teams to international competitions.
A kick full basket toss involves the flyer performing a kick at the top followed by a full twist before being caught.

A step-up involves a base stepping under a stunt to replace another base, all while the flyer stays elevated.

The National Cheerleading Day is celebrated on March 1 each year, recognizing the contributions of cheerleaders at all levels.

Flyers typically weigh between 90 and 130 pounds, though there is no official weight limit in competitive cheerleading rules.

The concept of 'clean' in competitive cheer means sharp, precise movements with no wobbles, bobbles, or timing errors.

Cheerleading mats for competitions are typically 2 inches thick to provide adequate cushioning for tumbling and dismounts.
